generations.code:
import sys
from itertools import product

input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
idx = 0

def rd():
    global idx
    v = input_data[idx]; idx += 1
    return int(v)

t = rd()
for _ in range(t):
    n, m = rd(), rd()
    x = [rd() for _ in range(n)]
    s = []
    for i in range(n):
        row = input_data[idx].decode(); idx += 1
        s.append([int(c) for c in row])
    
    best_surprise = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for signs in range(1 << n):
        sigma = [1 if (signs >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)]
        w = [sum(sigma[i] * s[i][j] for i in range(n)) for j in range(m)]
        order = sorted(range(m), key=lambda j: w[j])
        p = [0] * m
        for rank, j in enumerate(order):
            p[j] = m - rank  # largest value to smallest w
        r = [sum(p[j] * s[i][j] for j in range(m)) for i in range(n)]
        surprise = sum(abs(x[i] - r[i]) for i in range(n))
        if surprise > best_surprise:
            best_surprise = surprise
            best_p = p[:]
    
    print(*best_p)

bugs.description: The algorithm enumerates all 2^n sign assignments and for each computes w, sorts, and recomputes r_i with nested loops, leading to O(2^n * n * m) operations plus sorting O(2^n * m log m). With n=10 and m up to 10^4 this results in over 200 million elementary operations per test case, which is too slow for Python and will likely cause TLE.
bugs.triggering_input: n=10, m=10000, with arbitrary s_i and x_i

bugs.description: The algorithm enumerates all 2^n sign assignments and for each computes w, sorts, and recomputes r_i with nested loops, leading to O(2^n * n * m) operations plus sorting O(2^n * m log m). With n=10 and m up to 10^4 this results in over 200 million elementary operations per test case, which is too slow for Python and will likely cause TLE.
bugs.triggering_input: n=10, m=10000, with arbitrary s_i and x_i

fixes.code:
import sys
import numpy as np

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0

    def rd():
        nonlocal idx
        v = input_data[idx]; idx += 1
        return int(v)

    t = rd()
    for _ in range(t):
        n, m = rd(), rd()
        x = [rd() for _ in range(n)]
        s = []
        for i in range(n):
            row = input_data[idx].decode(); idx += 1
            s.append([int(c) for c in row])
        
        s_arr = np.array(s, dtype=np.int32)  # n x m
        x_arr = np.array(x, dtype=np.int64)  # n
        
        best_surprise = -1
        best_p = None
        
        # Precompute ranks array: sorted order gives p values
        # For each sign assignment
        for signs in range(1 << n):
            sigma = np.array([1 if (signs >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)], dtype=np.int32)
            w = sigma @ s_arr  # shape: m
            # Sort columns by w ascending, assign p = m, m-1, ..., 1 to descending w
            order = np.argsort(w)  # ascending order of w
            p = np.empty(m, dtype=np.int64)
            # position order[0] gets value m (largest to largest w... wait)
            # We want largest p to largest w
            # order is ascending, so order[-1] has largest w -> gets p=m
            ranks = np.empty(m, dtype=np.int64)
            ranks[order] = np.arange(1, m+1, dtype=np.int64)  # order[0] gets 1, order[-1] gets m
            p = ranks
            
            r = s_arr @ p  # n
            surprise = int(np.sum(np.abs(x_arr - r)))
            if surprise > best_surprise:
                best_surprise = surprise
                best_p = p.copy()
        
        print(*best_p)

solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
import numpy as np

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0

    def rd():
        nonlocal idx
        v = input_data[idx]; idx += 1
        return int(v)

    t = rd()
    for _ in range(t):
        n, m = rd(), rd()
        x = [rd() for _ in range(n)]
        s = []
        for i in range(n):
            row = input_data[idx].decode(); idx += 1
            s.append([int(c) for c in row])
        
        s_arr = np.array(s, dtype=np.int32)  # n x m
        x_arr = np.array(x, dtype=np.int64)  # n
        
        best_surprise = -1
        best_p = None
        
        # Precompute ranks array: sorted order gives p values
        # For each sign assignment
        for signs in range(1 << n):
            sigma = np.array([1 if (signs >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)], dtype=np.int32)
            w = sigma @ s_arr  # shape: m
            # Sort columns by w ascending, assign p = m, m-1, ..., 1 to descending w
            order = np.argsort(w)  # ascending order of w
            p = np.empty(m, dtype=np.int64)
            # position order[0] gets value m (largest to largest w... wait)
            # We want largest p to largest w
            # order is ascending, so order[-1] has largest w -> gets p=m
            ranks = np.empty(m, dtype=np.int64)
            ranks[order] = np.arange(1, m+1, dtype=np.int64)  # order[0] gets 1, order[-1] gets m
            p = ranks
            
            r = s_arr @ p  # n
            surprise = int(np.sum(np.abs(x_arr - r)))
            if surprise > best_surprise:
                best_surprise = surprise
                best_p = p.copy()
        
        print(*best_p)

solve()
